header_info.title: Global Coal Plant Tracker, Global Energy Monitor  January 2026 Release
header_info.citation: Recommended Citation: "Global Energy Monitor, Global Coal Plant Tracker, January 2026 release."  (See the CC license for attribution requirements if sharing or adapting the data set.)
header_info.contact: Contact: Christine Shearer, Project Manager, Global Energy Monitor - christine.shearer@globalenergymonitor.org

entries.name: Announced
entries.definition: Projects that have appeared in corporate or governmental planning documents but have not yet moved actively forward by applying for permits or seeking land, coal, or financing.

entries.name: Pre-permit
entries.definition: Projects that have actively moved forward in one or more of the following ways: applying for environmental permits, acquiring land, acquiring coal, acquiring water rights, acquiring transmission arrangements, or securing financing. In India the key criterion for moving a project from "announced" to "pre-permit" is the existence of a "terms of reference" (TOR) letter by the Ministry of Environment, Forests, and Climate Change. In China, "pre-permit" means a feasibility study has been completed.

entries.name: Permitted
entries.definition: Projects that have secured all environmental permits but have not broken ground. In India the key criterion for moving a project from "pre-permit" to "permitted" is the approval of an "environmental clearance" (EC) by the Ministry of Environment, Forests, and Climate Change. In China, "permitted" means a plant has received a permit from the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC), allowing for construction, or the equivalent approval at the province level.

entries.name: Construction
entries.definition: Projects where physical construction (i.e. concrete and steel, not just a ground-breaking ceremony or early site preparation) has begun.

entries.name: Shelved
entries.definition: Projects where sufficient evidence is found to indicate that a project is no longer moving forward, but not enough to declare it definitively cancelled. Projects where construction has been put on hold are also considered "shelved." A project that shows no activity over a period of 2 years is categorized as "shelved" unless there is evidence to the contrary.

entries.name: Cancelled
entries.definition: In some cases a sponsor announces that it has cancelled a project. More often a project fails to advance and then quietly disappears from related documents. In addition, a plant that shows no activity or with no known developments over a period of 4 years is categorized as "cancelled" (or two years since being marked "shelved"), unless there is evidence to the contrary. Proposed projects that are switched to natural gas are considered "cancelled" as coal plants.

entries.name: Operating
entries.definition: Units that have been formally commissioned or that have entered commercial operation.

entries.name: Mothballed
entries.definition: Units that have been deactivated or put into an inactive state but are not retired.

entries.name: Retired
entries.definition: Units that have been permanently decommissioned or converted to another fuel.

entries.name: GEM unit/phase ID
entries.definition: ID unique to the coal-fired unit

entries.name: GEM location ID
entries.definition: ID unique to the entire power station

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: Country where the power station is/was located or is/was in development

entries.name: Wiki URL
entries.definition: Link to GEM's power station wiki page, featuring narrative descriptions and source attribution

entries.name: Plant name
entries.definition: Identical label across all units at the same power station

entries.name: Unit name
entries.definition: Unique label for each unit at the same power station

entries.name: Plant name (other)
entries.definition: Alternative and former labels for the power station

entries.name: Plant name (local)
entries.definition: Power station name in a local language or government plan

entries.name: Owner
entries.definition: Lowest level, direct power station owner(s)

entries.name: Owner GEM Entity ID
entries.definition: GEOT ID unique to the entity

entries.name: Parent
entries.definition: Highest level power station owner(s) and their share of ownership, if known

entries.name: Parent GEM Entity ID
entries.definition: GEOT ID unique to the parent or parent configuration

entries.name: Capacity (MW)
entries.definition: Nameplate/gross/installed capacity, in megawatts

entries.name: Status
entries.definition: Status designation, see more details above

entries.name: Start year
entries.definition: Year the unit was commissioned

entries.name: Retired year
entries.definition: Year the unit was decommissioned

entries.name: Planned retirement
entries.definition: Year a specific unit is planned to retire (specific, not national commitments)

entries.name: Phaseout commitment
entries.definition: Year of planned country-level coal phaseout

entries.name: Net-zero commitment
entries.definition: Year of country-level net-zero target

entries.name: Combustion technology
entries.definition: Power station technology type. Fore more info about each type, see https://www.gem.wiki/Coal_power_technologies

entries.name: Coal type
entries.definition: Anthracite, bituminous, lignite, subbituminous, waste coal, or unknown

entries.name: Coal source
entries.definition: Mine, region, or country of coal origin, to highest level of known specificity

entries.name: Conversion to (fuel)
entries.definition: Fuel details connected to another GEM tracker, used when a project conversion is planned

entries.name: Conversion to (GEM unit ID)
entries.definition: Specific ID connected to another GEM tracker, used when a project conversion is planned

entries.name: Alternate Fuel
entries.definition: Details about co-firing with another fuel, generally excluding any start-up fuels or cold-start fuels

entries.name: Location
entries.definition: Closest location (e.g. city or town)

entries.name: Local area
entries.definition: Taluk, county

entries.name: Major area
entries.definition: Prefecture, district

entries.name: Subnational unit
entries.definition: Province, state

entries.name: Subregion
entries.definition: Global subregion

entries.name: Region
entries.definition: Global region

entries.name: Latitude
entries.definition: WGS 84 Decimal latitude

entries.name: Longitude
entries.definition: WGS 84 Decimal longitude

entries.name: Location accuracy
entries.definition: Whether coordinates are exact or approximate

entries.name: Permits
entries.definition: Date the power station received environmental clearance

entries.name: Captive
entries.definition: Industry, if the power station is designated for paricular non-grid use

entries.name: Captive industry use
entries.definition: Whether a captive power station provides heat, power, or both

entries.name: Captive residential use
entries.definition: Whether the plant sell excess heat, power, or both

entries.name: CHP
entries.definition: Whether the unit is used for combined heat and power

entries.name: China capacity payment recipient
entries.definition: Whether the unit is eligible to receive payments under China's coal capacity payment mechanism. For more info see https://www.gem.wiki/China_Coal_Capacity_Payment_Mechanism

entries.name: Metholology
entries.definition: For CO2 calculation method, see "Estimating carbon dioxide emissions from coal plants" at https://www.gem.wiki/Estimating_carbon_dioxide_emissions_from_coal_plants

entries.name: Note
entries.definition: The GCPT estimates for CO2 emissions are intended to provide guidance on overall country and regional emissions over the lifecycle of the existing and proposed fleet of plants. They are not intended to provide detailed impact analysis for particular plants. For this reason, the average capacity factor by country is used for all plants. In general, newer plants will tend to have higher capacity factors than the average, and older plants will have lower capacity factors than the average. Similarly, heat rates and emission factors represent broad performance; these factors will differ for particular plants and coal supplies.

entries.name: Capacity factor
entries.definition: Average capacity factor by country, based on GEM's global operating coal power capacity (2025) and Ember's power generation data (accessed 23 January 2026). Plants that are retired or cancelled are given a capacity factor of 0, i.e. no generation is assumed. Capacity data for Indonesia includes grid plants only, as the generation data excludes captive plants. Because the GCPT excludes coal-fired units under 30 MW, the average capacity factor reported here likely overstates national averages in countries with a non-trivial share of small units.

entries.name: Plant age
entries.definition: The age of the plant. For operating and mothballed plants it is the number of years since the plant began operating. For retired plants it is the age that the plant retired. Age is set to 0 for proposed plants and for plants that have been operating less than a year.

entries.name: Lifetime
entries.definition: Lifetime emissions assume a plant lifetime of 35 years, based on the global average retirement age of 37 years. Plants older than 35 years are assumed to operate an additional 5 years. Plants with a planned retirement date are assumed to cease operations that year.

entries.name: Heat rate
entries.definition: Heat rate is based on type of combustion technology, with performance penalties for older and smaller units. More discussion at https://www.gem.wiki/Estimating_carbon_dioxide_emissions_from_coal_plants

entries.name: Emission factor
entries.definition: Based on type of coal using the 2006 IPPC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Inventories, Table 2.2, page 2.16.
